title: Chapter Sixty: The Sword Dragon Returns
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Xuzhou had more vigor than usual.

Fresh rumors spread fast -- a young Desolate master had provoked the Blood Flame Sect. Not a white rank but a blue-ranked master, they said. The story gained force with every telling. By morning the entire city knew.

Yeon-shin stayed at the inn next to the pleasure house. He did not seek sleep in luxury. He thought, if it was the Blood Flame Sect, they would find him wherever he was in Xuzhou now.

He was bait, and he knew it. The same way the Blood Flame Sect had used Won-chang as bait.

*The difference is that I chose this.*

---

"We greet the renowned Desolate Sect warrior."

Three martial artists stood across from him at breakfast. One woman and two men, all with swords at their waists. They called themselves Seol Yu-yu of the Eulso Sword Family, Jeong Cheol of the Flower Sword Gate, and Kwon Chun of the Flying Dragon Martial Hall. Eldest daughter, young sect leader, and top disciple.

*No goodwill in their expressions.*

He recalled the social skills learned from Hyeon Won-chang. The memory was a knife's edge, but he used it anyway.

*Praise the opponent if you need something. Not excessively.*

"It is an honor to meet the heroes of South Zhili," Yeon-shin said, bowing his head slightly.

Kwon Chun's narrow brow relaxed. A blue-ranked master's greeting with such courteous bearing -- it was not what they had expected.

"Knowing our martial arts are greatly lacking," Kwon Chun said, clearing his throat, "would you consider having a sword discussion with us?"

Sword discussion. Virtual matches spoken through words, not blades. Yeon-shin had lost to Won-chang at these many times -- the man could describe techniques he could never physically demonstrate.

*This would be good for matching their mood.*

He ordered three bottles of alcohol and side dishes. Lost continuously. Deliberately, gracefully, as Won-chang had taught him. Sword discussion was a war of words, and the side with weaker will to win had to lose. Yeon-shin naturally learned about life in this setting.

It felt almost too easy, this entertaining of guests.

The three people's expressions brightened with every verbal victory against a Desolate blue-ranked master. Their tongues loosened with alcohol and pride. Yeon-shin asked careful questions, and the answers came freely.

Where they had recently seen people hiding their appearance. What kinds of hushed talks circulated about Xuzhou's Blood Flame Sect. Where they had met those fellows. These three were rising talents of this city -- their access to information and discernment far surpassed government soldiers.

Yeon-shin gathered every useful story. The three had even followed the Blood Flame members he had let live yesterday, tracking them to their hideouts. He could organize the places that needed shattering.

Then --

"Sloppy? How could that be?" A clear voice cut through the drunken chatter, pushing away the haze of alcohol like a cold wind. "He newly created sword techniques as profound as the Nine Sects' secret arts."

Yeon-shin felt the presence only as the voice arrived. That alone told him everything.

He turned his head.

Beautiful features. Clear eyes. The feather ornament above black hair, unchanged from the day they had met. Sky-blue clothes fluttering from the rebound of light body technique. A white precious sword hung from a waist sash decorated with blue jade -- the divine sword signifying her status as successor to the Zhongnan Sect's leader.

"Great Hero Sword Dragon?"

Wei Ji Myo-hwa raised her lips slightly. A smile that left the three dining companions staring with dazed expressions.

"I was coming to participate in the Dragon Phoenix Gathering," she said. "I came up hearing rising talents were being chased." Her smile deepened. "Since rumors about Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Flash are rampant in Xuzhou."

"You came looking directly?"

"They say you killed a Blood Swordmaster in one move. The rumors must have spread faster than my lightness technique." She paused. "Enough to make it incomprehensible that the Blood Flame Sect stays quiet."

"They must have schemes. Though happy to meet again, I have no leisure to wait." He met her eyes. "Hyeon Won-chang was captured."

"Ah."

A short sigh. She nodded.

"I will help. The Zhongnan Sect owes a debt to the two young heroes."

He gained an army in the early evening. Yeon-shin did not refuse. After paying the innkeeper, he left with Wei Ji Myo-hwa, leaving behind the three martial artists who were muttering "Sword Dragon" and "Zhongnan Sect" with dazed expressions.

"We will need to track for quite a long time. Will this be alright?"

She twisted her body slightly, showing her traveling kit. One sword and a small pack on her back. Very simple. Worthy of a Nine Sects disciple.

Yeon-shin shook his head. "We might not need long."

---

The pursuit gained rapid momentum.

At this moment, his upper dantian precognition was flashing like lightning from clear sky. Whether reaction from widening the Baihui point through the forced sword song, or whether Wei Ji Myo-hwa's mysterious energy beside him provided stimulus -- he could not say.

But every place they entered was a Blood Flame Sect branch.

*Kwang!*

Eternal Blossom Fist's Advancing Thunder shattered a gate. Jeong Yeon-shin stood amid the flying fragments, sky-blue spirit light blazing around him.

*Kuung!*

The Azure Unyielding Palm stormed through a branch's inner house. He struck down the last man's head and moved on.

Five branches. Then six. Information gathered from government offices and martial artists harmonized with his precognition, and the tracking speed was something beyond reason -- possessed, relentless, unerring.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa had no chance to intervene. His short-distance lightness technique was fast enough to rival hers, but the Azure Unyielding Palm was beyond even that. It displayed might reaching invincibility against the Blood Flame Sect. Every branch fell the same way -- doors shattered, blood demons broken, and Yeon-shin moving on before the dust had settled.

*Just do not meet an Apostle.*

He recalled the Blood Flame Sect's Seventh Apostle -- the one with grudges against him. Heaven's energy passing through his crown whispered, annoyingly, that there was no need to worry today.

Whether that was truly so remained to be seen.

A Desolate Sect blue-ranked warrior rushed through Xuzhou together with a beautiful master of mysterious age. Bold, conspicuous, uncaring of eyes. At the end of their actions, completely shattered Blood Flame branches were revealed to all under heaven. Victims withered from true qi extraction. Corpses with bright red hair.

"Crazy -- they said Desolate Sect, Desolate Sect."

"Is that not simply a monster?"

"Watch your words. That is a great hero."

The gathering crowd grew larger with each branch they destroyed. Xuzhou had many martial gates, and the people who came out wondering what was happening followed one by one. More and more.

*Four branches remain.*

Information and precognition pointed toward the final hidden bases. Somewhere in those bases, Won-chang might be waiting. Might be alive. Might still have time.

The pressure at his crown pulsed. He ignored it. His hands were steady. His purpose was clear.

And the crowd behind him grew like a river swelling after rain, following the path of destruction through Xuzhou's streets, murmuring one name --

*Lightning Flash.*
